content.outcomes_html: Define voltage, current, and resistance and apply V = I x R to simple circuits.
content.explain_html: Use one worked example for each variable rearrangement. Highlight units and decimal conversion.
content.practice_html:
• Calculate current from voltage and resistance.
  • Calculate resistance from voltage and current.
content.assessment_html: Exit ticket: 3 mixed Ohm's Law questions with one real-world appliance context.

content.outcomes_html: Explain current flow and total resistance in series circuits.
content.explain_html: Demonstrate that current is constant in a series path and resistance adds across components.
content.practice_html: Build two series circuits and compare measured vs calculated values.
content.assessment_html: Short quiz with 5 series-resistance calculations.

content.outcomes_html: Describe voltage and current behaviour in parallel circuits.
content.explain_html: Show equal branch voltage and current split across unequal branch resistances.
content.practice_html: Construct a two-branch circuit and record branch current readings.
content.assessment_html: Compare two circuit diagrams and justify which has lower equivalent resistance.

content.outcomes_html: Apply Kirchhoff's Current Law and Voltage Law to one-loop and two-loop examples.
content.explain_html: Use node and loop equations with clear sign convention steps.
content.practice_html: Solve two guided problems, then check by summing volt drops around each loop.
content.assessment_html: Independent problem: derive unknown branch current using KCL.

content.outcomes_html: Calculate electrical power and energy usage for simple loads.
content.explain_html: Introduce P = V x I and E = P x t with unit conversions.
content.practice_html: Estimate daily energy use for three classroom devices.
content.assessment_html: Homework: cost comparison using tariff rates and usage profiles.

content.outcomes_html: Use a digital multimeter safely to measure voltage, current, and resistance.
content.explain_html: Demonstrate range selection, probe placement, and correct meter mode.
content.practice_html: Practical rotations measuring five pre-wired test points.
content.assessment_html: Observed checklist: each learner completes one safe measurement sequence.

content.outcomes_html: Identify electrical hazards and describe a safe isolation process.
content.explain_html: Walk through lockout principles, test-before-touch, and PPE minimums.
content.practice_html: Scenario cards: pick safe actions for each workshop situation.
content.assessment_html: 10-question safety check requiring 80 percent pass threshold.

content.outcomes_html: Differentiate MCB, fuse, and RCD operating principles.
content.explain_html: Use fault scenarios to show which protective device should operate first.
content.practice_html: Match circuit faults to appropriate protective devices and ratings.
content.assessment_html: Written response: justify protective selection for a small workshop circuit.

content.outcomes_html: Use a repeatable fault-finding process on low-voltage circuits.
content.explain_html: Teach confirm-symptom, isolate-section, test, and verify-fix workflow.
content.practice_html: Timed diagnostic exercise with one hidden open-circuit and one short-circuit fault.
content.assessment_html: Learners submit fault logs with evidence and final corrective action.

content.outcomes_html: Explain basic switching logic using relays and contact states.
content.explain_html: Compare normally-open and normally-closed contacts in control circuits.
content.practice_html: Wire a start-stop relay circuit and test latching behaviour.
content.assessment_html: Draw and annotate a simple switching circuit from memory.

content.outcomes_html: Classify common engineering materials and relate properties to product selection.
content.explain_html: Compare strength, toughness, hardness, and corrosion resistance for metals, polymers, and composites.
content.practice_html: Match component case studies to suitable material choices with justification.
content.assessment_html: Short written task: select materials for three workshop applications and explain trade-offs.

content.outcomes_html: Use vernier calipers and micrometers to measure components to tolerance.
content.explain_html: Introduce nominal size, tolerance bands, and basic geometric tolerance interpretation.
content.practice_html: Measure a sample batch of parts and record pass/fail against drawing limits.
content.assessment_html: Practical check: complete a measurement sheet with correct units and tolerances.

content.outcomes_html: Identify thread forms and apply correct torque methods during assembly.
content.explain_html: Cover bolt grades, thread pitch, and why preload and torque sequence matter.
content.practice_html: Assemble a small jointed fixture using torque settings and sequence instructions.
content.assessment_html: Checklist assessment on correct tool choice, torque value, and tightening pattern.

content.outcomes_html: Create a basic parametric part model and produce a simple engineering drawing.
content.explain_html: Demonstrate sketch constraints, feature creation, and drawing annotation basics.
content.practice_html: Model a bracket from dimensions, then export a drawing with key dimensions.
content.assessment_html: Submit model and drawing for review against feature and dimension criteria.

content.outcomes_html: Explain speed, torque, and ratio relationships for gears, belts, and chain drives.
content.explain_html: Use ratio equations and efficiency losses to compare transmission options.
content.practice_html: Set up drive combinations and calculate output speed from measured inputs.
content.assessment_html: Problem set: choose suitable drive systems for three machine scenarios.

content.outcomes_html: Describe core fluid power components and basic control circuit behavior.
content.explain_html: Introduce pressure, flow, actuators, and directional control valve functions.
content.practice_html: Build a simple actuator circuit and document sequence of operation.
content.assessment_html: Fault scenario quiz on leaks, pressure loss, and valve misconfiguration.

content.outcomes_html: Create a basic preventive maintenance schedule for rotating equipment.
content.explain_html: Cover maintenance intervals, critical assets, and recordkeeping standards.
content.practice_html: Draft a weekly and monthly checklist for bearings, belts, and lubrication points.
content.assessment_html: Review task: identify missing controls in a sample maintenance plan.

content.outcomes_html: Apply inspection plans and basic SPC concepts to manufactured parts.
content.explain_html: Introduce sampling, control limits, and common defect categorization methods.
content.practice_html: Inspect a sample lot and plot measured values against tolerance limits.
content.assessment_html: Mini report: recommend corrective actions from inspection findings.

content.outcomes_html: Explain CNC workflow from setup to verification and safe machine operation.
content.explain_html: Cover coordinate systems, tool offsets, and dry-run checks before cutting.
content.practice_html: Simulate a simple machining sequence and verify toolpath against drawing.
content.assessment_html: Safety-focused checklist and short test on CNC setup sequence.

content.outcomes_html: Recognize workshop hazards and apply safe working procedures.
content.explain_html: Discuss PPE, machine guarding, isolation procedures, and incident reporting.
content.practice_html: Complete a hazard-spotting walk-through with control measures for each risk.
content.assessment_html: Safety sign-off quiz requiring correct responses to practical scenarios.
